summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is an inequality?

An inequality compares two expressions and states that one is less than, greater than, less than or equal to, or greater than or equal to the other.

What is 'interval notation'?

Interval notation uses parentheses for excluded endpoints and brackets for included ones. For example, x > 3 is written as (3, infinity).

A movie theater charges $12 per ticket. A group has $100 to spend. The inequality 12t <= 100 represents the number of tickets they can buy. What is the maximum number of whole tickets?

Divide by 12: t <= 8.33. Since tickets must be whole numbers, the maximum is 8 tickets.

A student's final grade is the average of 4 tests. Scores so far: 82, 90, 78. The student needs at least an 85 average. The inequality (82 + 90 + 78 + x)/4 >= 85 gives the minimum score on the 4th test. What is it?

Multiply by 4: 250 + x >= 340. Subtract 250: x >= 90.

A budget-conscious shopper has $60 for school supplies. Binders cost $5 each and she already spent $15 on pens. The inequality 5b + 15 <= 60 models her budget. What is the maximum number of binders?

Subtract 15: 5b <= 45. Divide by 5: b <= 9 binders.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: When you multiply or divide both sides of an inequality by a negative number, you must:

Multiplying or dividing by a negative number reverses the order of the inequality. This is because multiplying by a negative flips the number line.

Example: A student needs at least a 70 on a test to pass. If the equation for the score is 5x + 20 >= 70, how many questions (x) must the student answer correctly?

Subtract 20: 5x >= 50. Divide by 5: x >= 10 questions.

Example: A student's final grade is the average of 4 tests. Scores so far: 82, 90, 78. The student needs at least an 85 average. The inequality (82 + 90 + 78 + x)/4 >= 85 gives the minimum score on the 4th test. What is it?

Multiply by 4: 250 + x >= 340. Subtract 250: x >= 90.
